history: 57-year-old woman diagnosed with a pituitary macroadenoma
History of urticaria caused by pollen and Computed Tomography contrast agent
presentation: Immediately after administration of rocuronium, pulse rate increased to 120 beats/min and blood pressure dropped to 77/36 mmHg
Generalized urticaria and tongue edema were observed
diagnostics: Rocuronium confirmed as the cause by skin prick test four days after the episode
Cisatracurium showed a negative reaction in the skin prick test
management: Epinephrine was administered and the surgery was postponed
Reoperation was planned two weeks after the event
Cisatracurium was selected as an alternative agent for future procedures
outcome: Patient underwent reoperation without any adverse events
Patient was discharged without any complications 7 days later
